fields.indication_summary: Calcium channel blocker indicated, alone or in combination with other antihypertensive and antianginal agents, for the treatment of hypertension (to lower blood pressure) and coronary artery disease: chronic stable angina, vasospastic angina (Prinzmetal's or variant angina), and angiographically documented CAD in patients without heart failure or ejection fraction < 40%.

notes: PI and SPL 'Revised:' dates agree (1/2019), but there is a THIRD, older date embedded mid-document: the patient-information insert carries its own footer 'LAB-0015-10.0 Revised March 2017'. It is FDA-approved patient labeling, not a formal Medication Guide, so medication_guide stays null and the date is recorded here instead. Six package NDCs across three strengths (0069-1520-68; 0069-1530-68/-41/-72; 0069-1540-68/-41); no single value can represent that, so ndc left null. Extraction quirk worth knowing: the HIGHLIGHTS Adverse Reactions text contains a literal 'Error! Hyperlink reference not valid.' artifact carried over from the source document authoring tool -- it is genuinely in the extracted text, not an extraction bug.
